---
title: Naive Bayes Claassifier
---

Prinsip dasar **Naive Bayes classifier** adalah: *mengklasifikasikan objek dengan membandingkan seberapa “mungkin” data itu muncul pada tiap kelas*, lalu memilih kelas dengan probabilitas posterior terbesar.

### 1) Bayes sebagai mesin “update keyakinan”

Naive Bayes memakai Teorema Bayes:

[
P(C\mid x)\propto P(C),P(x\mid C)
]

* **Prior** (P(C)): seberapa sering kelas itu terjadi sebelum melihat data (mis. spam cuma 1%).
* **Likelihood** (P(x\mid C)): seberapa cocok data (x) jika kelasnya (C).
* **Posterior** (P(C\mid x)): keyakinan setelah melihat data.

Kita tidak perlu menghitung penyebut (P(x)) karena sama untuk semua kelas, jadi cukup membandingkan skor:
[
\text{score}(C)=P(C),P(x\mid C)
]

---

### 2) Asumsi “naive”: fitur dianggap independen bersyarat pada kelas

Agar (P(x\mid C)) mudah dihitung, Naive Bayes membuat asumsi:

[
P(x_1,x_2,\dots,x_d\mid C)\approx \prod_{j=1}^d P(x_j\mid C)
]

Artinya: **setelah kelas diketahui**, tiap fitur dianggap tidak saling mempengaruhi.

Contoh:

* Spam detection: kata “gratis”, “promo”, “klik” dianggap independen *dengan syarat* email itu spam/ham.
* Credit scoring: fitur seperti DTI, income bin, purpose dianggap independen *dengan syarat* borrower good/bad.

Ini memang “naive” (di dunia nyata fitur sering berkorelasi), tapi sering tetap bekerja baik.

---

### 3) Keputusan klasifikasi: pilih kelas dengan posterior terbesar

[
\hat{C} = \arg\max_C \left[\log P(C) + \sum_j \log P(x_j\mid C)\right]
]

Biasanya dipakai **log** supaya:

* tidak underflow (perkalian angka kecil jadi 0),
* berubah jadi penjumlahan.

---

### 4) Intuisi versi “LLR” (Sherlock version)

Untuk binary class (1 vs 0), yang dibandingkan adalah **log-likelihood ratio**:

[
\log\frac{P(1\mid x)}{P(0\mid x)}
=================================

\log\frac{P(1)}{P(0)}
+
\sum_j \log\frac{P(x_j\mid 1)}{P(x_j\mid 0)}
]

* prior memberi “bias awal”
* tiap fitur memberi “bukti” pro/kon kelas 1

---

### 5) Kenapa perlu smoothing (Laplace)

Jika ada fitur yang **tidak pernah muncul** di kelas tertentu saat training, maka (P(x_j\mid C)=0) dan seluruh skor jadi 0 (log jadi (-\infty)).
Solusinya: **Laplace smoothing** (tambah (\alpha)) agar tidak ada probabilitas nol.

---

### 6) Kapan Naive Bayes cocok

* Data teks (spam, sentiment): sangat cepat dan baseline kuat.
* Data tabular diskret: oke jika fitur tidak terlalu saling terikat.
* Dataset besar/fitur banyak: bagus karena training sederhana.

Kalau Bapak mau, saya bisa jelaskan juga **tiga varian utama** (GaussianNB, MultinomialNB, BernoulliNB) dan kapan dipakai.

Baik Prof. Armein — kita masuk lebih dalam 👨‍🔬
Berikut penjelasan **tiga varian utama Naive Bayes**, lengkap dengan:

* intuisi matematis
* kapan dipakai
* contoh aplikatif (spam, sentiment, credit scoring)
* kelebihan & keterbatasan

---

# 1️⃣ Gaussian Naive Bayes

## 🔹 Asumsi

Setiap fitur numerik mengikuti distribusi **Gaussian (Normal)** dalam setiap kelas:

[
x_j \mid C \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_{Cj}, \sigma_{Cj}^2)
]

Likelihood:

[
P(x_j \mid C)
=============

\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi\sigma^2}}
\exp\left(-\frac{(x_j-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)
]

---

## 🔹 Kapan Dipakai?

✔ Fitur numerik kontinu
✔ Credit scoring (income, DTI, umur, dll)
✔ Sensor data
✔ Fraud detection berbasis angka

---

## 🔹 Contoh P2P Lending

Fitur:

* income (numeric)
* DTI ratio
* loan history months

Jika borrower GOOD punya income rata-rata 12 juta dan BAD rata-rata 4 juta, maka income tinggi otomatis meningkatkan likelihood kelas GOOD.

---

## 🔹 Kelebihan

* Tidak perlu binning
* Cepat
* Parameter sedikit (mean & variance per fitur per kelas)

## 🔹 Kekurangan

* Jika distribusi tidak mendekati normal → performa turun
* Sensitif terhadap outlier

---

# 2️⃣ Multinomial Naive Bayes

## 🔹 Asumsi

Fitur adalah **count/frequency** (biasanya teks)

[
P(x \mid C) = \prod_j P(w_j \mid C)^{count(w_j)}
]

---

## 🔹 Kapan Dipakai?

✔ Spam detection
✔ Sentiment analysis
✔ Document classification
✔ Text mining

---

## 🔹 Contoh Spam

Kata:

* “gratis” sangat sering muncul di spam
* “rapat” jarang muncul di spam

LLR untuk kata "gratis":

[
\log \frac{P(gratis\mid spam)}{P(gratis\mid ham)}
]

Jika besar → kuat mendukung spam.

---

## 🔹 Kelebihan

* Sangat efektif untuk teks
* Training sangat cepat
* Tahan terhadap dimensi tinggi

## 🔹 Kekurangan

* Asumsi independensi kata tidak realistis
* Tidak mempertimbangkan urutan kata (kecuali n-gram)

---

# 3️⃣ Bernoulli Naive Bayes

## 🔹 Asumsi

Fitur adalah **binary (0/1)** → hanya memperhatikan apakah fitur muncul atau tidak.

[
P(x_j\mid C)=p^{x_j}(1-p)^{1-x_j}
]

---

## 🔹 Kapan Dipakai?

✔ Text classification (presence/absence)
✔ Fitur boolean
✔ Sistem rule-based sederhana

---

## 🔹 Perbedaan dengan Multinomial

| Multinomial              | Bernoulli               |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------- |
| Menghitung frekuensi     | Hanya hadir/tidak       |
| Bagus untuk teks panjang | Bagus untuk teks pendek |
| Umum dipakai di NLP      | Lebih jarang            |

---

# 🎯 Perbandingan Singkat

| Tipe Data        | Model         |
| ---------------- | ------------- |
| Numerik kontinu  | GaussianNB    |
| Count teks       | MultinomialNB |
| Binary/boolean   | BernoulliNB   |
| Kategori diskret | CategoricalNB |

---

# 🔬 Intuisi Unified (Very Important)

Semua varian adalah bentuk khusus dari:

[
\hat{C}
=======

\arg\max_C
\left[
\log P(C)
+
\sum_j \log P(x_j\mid C)
\right]
]

Perbedaannya hanya pada bentuk (P(x_j\mid C)).

---

# 💡 Insight yang Kuat untuk Mahasiswa

Naive Bayes bukan “model statistik biasa”.
Ia adalah:

> **Mesin akumulasi bukti (evidence accumulation engine).**

Setiap fitur memberikan kontribusi:

[
\text{Total Evidence} = \text{Prior Evidence} + \sum \text{Feature Evidence}
]

Inilah alasan versi LLR sangat powerful untuk mengajar.

---

# 🚀 Untuk Konteks Kuliah Prof (Sherlock Holmes 2.0)

Saya sarankan struktur ini:

1. Spam → MultinomialNB
2. Sentiment → Bernoulli vs Multinomial comparison
3. P2P → GaussianNB vs DiscreteNB comparison
4. Diskusi imbalance & prior
5. Geser threshold → precision/recall tradeoff

---

Kalau Prof mau, saya bisa lanjut dengan:

* 🔎 Visualisasi kontribusi tiap fitur (feature importance berbasis LLR)
* ⚖ Demo imbalance ekstrem (base-rate fallacy)
* 📊 Plot decision boundary GaussianNB
* 🧠 Perbandingan Naive Bayes vs Logistic Regression (karena LLR bentuknya mirip)

Mau ke arah mana kita perdalam?

```python
import re
from collections import Counter

def tokenize(text):
    # Lowercase + ambil kata
    return re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+", text.lower())

def train_keyword_model(emails, labels):
    """
    emails: list[str]
    labels: list[int]  # 1 spam, 0 ham
    return counts kata per kelas + prior
    """
    spam_words = Counter()
    ham_words = Counter()
    n_spam = 0
    n_ham = 0

    for text, y in zip(emails, labels):
        words = tokenize(text)
        if y == 1:
            n_spam += 1
            spam_words.update(words)
        else:
            n_ham += 1
            ham_words.update(words)

    prior_spam = n_spam / (n_spam + n_ham) if (n_spam + n_ham) else 0.0
    return {"spam_words": spam_words, "ham_words": ham_words, "prior_spam": prior_spam}

def predict_naive_keyword(text, model, keywords=("gratis", "promo", "diskon"), threshold=1):
    """
    SANGAT naive: jika jumlah keyword muncul >= threshold => spam
    """
    words = tokenize(text)
    c = Counter(words)
    score = sum(c[k] for k in keywords)
    return 1 if score >= threshold else 0, score

# Demo mini
emails = [
    "Gratis diskon besar untuk kamu!",
    "Rapat jam 10, jangan lupa agenda",
    "Promo terbatas! gratis ongkir",
    "Tolong kirim laporan minggu ini"
]
labels = [1, 0, 1, 0]

model = train_keyword_model(emails, labels)
print("Prior spam:", model["prior_spam"])

for e in emails:
    yhat, score = predict_naive_keyword(e, model)
    print(yhat, score, "=>", e)
```

Di bawah ini saya berikan **dua versi Naive Bayes classifier** untuk **3 aplikasi**:

1. **Spam detection (teks)**
2. **Sentiment analysis (teks)**
3. **Credit scoring P2P lending (tabular)**

Versi:

* **A. Tanpa sklearn** (pure Python + NumPy)
* **B. Dengan sklearn**

Semua contoh **self-contained** (langsung jalan), lengkap dengan **train → predict → confusion matrix**.

---

## Utilitas umum: confusion matrix + metrik (dipakai semua contoh)

```python
import numpy as np

def confusion_2x2(y_true, y_pred, positive_label=1):
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_pred = np.asarray(y_pred)

    TP = int(np.sum((y_true == positive_label) & (y_pred == positive_label)))
    TN = int(np.sum((y_true != positive_label) & (y_pred != positive_label)))
    FP = int(np.sum((y_true != positive_label) & (y_pred == positive_label)))
    FN = int(np.sum((y_true == positive_label) & (y_pred != positive_label)))

    cm = np.array([[TN, FP],
                   [FN, TP]])
    return cm, TP, FP, TN, FN

def metrics_from_cm(TP, FP, TN, FN):
    precision = TP / (TP + FP) if (TP + FP) else 0.0
    recall    = TP / (TP + FN) if (TP + FN) else 0.0
    accuracy  = (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN) if (TP+TN+FP+FN) else 0.0
    f1 = (2*precision*recall)/(precision+recall) if (precision+recall) else 0.0
    return {"precision": precision, "recall": recall, "accuracy": accuracy, "f1": f1}
```

---

# A) TANPA sklearn

## A1) Naive Bayes untuk teks (Multinomial NB) — core engine

Dipakai untuk **spam detection** dan **sentiment analysis**.

```python
import re
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

def tokenize(text):
    return re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+", text.lower())

class MultinomialNB_Text_NoSK:
    """
    Multinomial Naive Bayes untuk teks:
    P(class|doc) ∝ P(class) * Π P(word|class)^{count(word,doc)}
    dengan Laplace smoothing.
    """
    def __init__(self, alpha=1.0):
        self.alpha = float(alpha)
        self.class_priors_log = {}
        self.word_logprob = {}   # dict[class] -> dict[word] -> log P(word|class)
        self.vocab = set()
        self.classes_ = None

    def fit(self, texts, y):
        y = list(y)
        texts = list(texts)
        classes = sorted(set(y))
        self.classes_ = classes

        # Count words per class
        word_counts = {c: Counter() for c in classes}
        class_counts = Counter(y)

        for text, label in zip(texts, y):
            words = tokenize(text)
            word_counts[label].update(words)
            self.vocab.update(words)

        V = len(self.vocab)

        # log priors
        n = len(y)
        self.class_priors_log = {c: np.log(class_counts[c] / n) for c in classes}

        # log likelihoods with Laplace smoothing
        self.word_logprob = {}
        for c in classes:
            total_words_in_class = sum(word_counts[c].values())
            denom = total_words_in_class + self.alpha * V
            self.word_logprob[c] = {}
            for w in self.vocab:
                num = word_counts[c][w] + self.alpha
                self.word_logprob[c][w] = np.log(num / denom)

            # store unseen word logprob (for words outside vocab during predict)
            self.word_logprob[c]["<UNK>"] = np.log(self.alpha / denom)

        return self

    def predict_proba(self, texts):
        probs = []
        for text in texts:
            words = tokenize(text)
            counts = Counter(words)
            log_scores = []
            for c in self.classes_:
                s = self.class_priors_log[c]
                for w, k in counts.items():
                    s += k * self.word_logprob[c].get(w, self.word_logprob[c]["<UNK>"])
                log_scores.append(s)

            # softmax
            log_scores = np.array(log_scores)
            log_scores = log_scores - np.max(log_scores)
            p = np.exp(log_scores)
            p = p / p.sum()
            probs.append(p)
        return np.array(probs)

    def predict(self, texts):
        proba = self.predict_proba(texts)
        idx = np.argmax(proba, axis=1)
        return np.array([self.classes_[i] for i in idx])
```

---

## A2) (1) Spam Detection — tanpa sklearn

```python
# Dataset kecil (demo). Untuk kelas, 1=spam, 0=ham
spam_texts = [
    "Gratis hadiah besar klik link sekarang",
    "Promo diskon 90 persen hanya hari ini",
    "Selamat kamu menang voucher belanja",
    "Pinjaman cepat cair tanpa syarat klik",
    "Dapatkan uang gratis sekarang juga",
    "Claim hadiah kamu melalui link ini",
]
ham_texts = [
    "Rapat jam 10 besok di ruang meeting",
    "Tolong kirim laporan mingguan hari ini",
    "Jadwal kuliah diundur jadi jam 13",
    "Terima kasih atas kerja samanya",
    "Mari diskusi proposal penelitian minggu depan",
    "Saya sudah upload materi ke LMS",
]

X = spam_texts + ham_texts
y = [1]*len(spam_texts) + [0]*len(ham_texts)

# Split sederhana
idx = np.arange(len(X))
np.random.seed(0)
np.random.shuffle(idx)
train_idx, test_idx = idx[:9], idx[9:]

X_train = [X[i] for i in train_idx]
y_train = [y[i] for i in train_idx]
X_test  = [X[i] for i in test_idx]
y_test  = [y[i] for i in test_idx]

model_spam = MultinomialNB_Text_NoSK(alpha=1.0).fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = model_spam.predict(X_test)

cm, TP, FP, TN, FN = confusion_2x2(y_test, y_pred, positive_label=1)
print("=== Spam Detection (No sklearn) ===")
print("Confusion Matrix [[TN,FP],[FN,TP]]:\n", cm)
print("Metrics:", metrics_from_cm(TP, FP, TN, FN))

# Coba prediksi baru
samples = [
    "gratis voucher klik link",
    "mohon kirim revisi draft proposal",
]
print("Pred:", model_spam.predict(samples))
print("Proba:", model_spam.predict_proba(samples))
```

---

## A3) (2) Sentiment Analysis — tanpa sklearn

```python
# 1=positive, 0=negative (demo kecil)
pos = [
    "film ini bagus sekali dan sangat menyentuh",
    "saya suka layanan ini cepat dan ramah",
    "produk berkualitas dan pengiriman tepat waktu",
    "pengalaman luar biasa saya puas",
    "rekomendasi terbaik mantap",
]
neg = [
    "film ini buruk dan membosankan",
    "layanan lambat dan tidak membantu",
    "produk rusak dan mengecewakan",
    "pengalaman jelek saya kesal",
    "sangat tidak puas mengecewakan",
]

X = pos + neg
y = [1]*len(pos) + [0]*len(neg)

idx = np.arange(len(X))
np.random.seed(1)
np.random.shuffle(idx)
train_idx, test_idx = idx[:7], idx[7:]

X_train = [X[i] for i in train_idx]
y_train = [y[i] for i in train_idx]
X_test  = [X[i] for i in test_idx]
y_test  = [y[i] for i in test_idx]

model_sent = MultinomialNB_Text_NoSK(alpha=1.0).fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = model_sent.predict(X_test)

cm, TP, FP, TN, FN = confusion_2x2(y_test, y_pred, positive_label=1)
print("\n=== Sentiment Analysis (No sklearn) ===")
print("Confusion Matrix [[TN,FP],[FN,TP]]:\n", cm)
print("Metrics:", metrics_from_cm(TP, FP, TN, FN))

samples = [
    "saya puas sekali layanan ramah",
    "produk ini mengecewakan dan buruk",
]
print("Pred:", model_sent.predict(samples))
print("Proba:", model_sent.predict_proba(samples))
```

---

## A4) (3) Credit Scoring P2P Lending — tanpa sklearn (Discrete Naive Bayes)

Untuk tabular, cara paling aman tanpa sklearn: **diskritisasi fitur numerik** → lalu **Naive Bayes diskret** (kategori).

```python
class DiscreteNB_NoSK:
    """
    Naive Bayes untuk fitur diskret/kategori.
    P(y|x) ∝ P(y) * Π P(x_j = v | y)
    Laplace smoothing per fitur.
    """
    def __init__(self, alpha=1.0):
        self.alpha = float(alpha)
        self.classes_ = None
        self.prior_log = {}
        self.cond_log = {}   # cond_log[y][j][value] = log prob
        self.feature_values_ = []  # list of sets

    def fit(self, X, y):
        X = np.asarray(X, dtype=object)
        y = np.asarray(y, dtype=object)
        n, d = X.shape
        classes = sorted(set(y))
        self.classes_ = classes

        # nilai unik per fitur
        self.feature_values_ = [set(X[:, j]) for j in range(d)]

        # prior
        counts_y = Counter(y)
        self.prior_log = {c: np.log(counts_y[c] / n) for c in classes}

        # conditional counts
        self.cond_log = {c: [defaultdict(float) for _ in range(d)] for c in classes}

        for c in classes:
            Xc = X[y == c]
            for j in range(d):
                vals = list(self.feature_values_[j])
                counts = Counter(Xc[:, j])
                V = len(vals)
                denom = len(Xc) + self.alpha * V
                for v in vals:
                    num = counts.get(v, 0) + self.alpha
                    self.cond_log[c][j][v] = np.log(num / denom)
                # unseen:
                self.cond_log[c][j]["<UNK>"] = np.log(self.alpha / denom)

        return self

    def predict_proba(self, X):
        X = np.asarray(X, dtype=object)
        n, d = X.shape
        probs = np.zeros((n, len(self.classes_)), dtype=float)

        for i in range(n):
            log_scores = []
            for c in self.classes_:
                s = self.prior_log[c]
                for j in range(d):
                    v = X[i, j]
                    s += self.cond_log[c][j].get(v, self.cond_log[c][j]["<UNK>"])
                log_scores.append(s)

            log_scores = np.array(log_scores)
            log_scores = log_scores - np.max(log_scores)
            p = np.exp(log_scores)
            p = p / p.sum()
            probs[i, :] = p
        return probs

    def predict(self, X):
        proba = self.predict_proba(X)
        idx = np.argmax(proba, axis=1)
        return np.array([self.classes_[i] for i in idx])

def bin_income(x):
    # juta rupiah/bulan (contoh)
    if x < 5: return "low"
    if x < 12: return "mid"
    return "high"

def bin_dti(x):
    # debt-to-income ratio (0..1)
    if x < 0.2: return "low"
    if x < 0.5: return "mid"
    return "high"

def bin_history_months(m):
    if m < 6: return "short"
    if m < 24: return "mid"
    return "long"

# Dataset demo P2P lending:
# fitur: income_bin, dti_bin, history_bin, purpose
# label: 1=good (lancar), 0=bad (default)
raw = [
    (10, 0.25, 18, "working_capital", 1),
    (3,  0.60, 4,  "consumption",     0),
    (7,  0.35, 8,  "education",       1),
    (4,  0.55, 10, "consumption",     0),
    (15, 0.15, 36, "investment",      1),
    (6,  0.70, 5,  "working_capital", 0),
    (9,  0.40, 30, "working_capital", 1),
    (2,  0.80, 3,  "consumption",     0),
    (13, 0.30, 20, "investment",      1),
    (5,  0.45, 12, "education",       1),
    (8,  0.65, 7,  "consumption",     0),
    (12, 0.22, 28, "working_capital", 1),
]

X = []
y = []
for income, dti, hist, purpose, label in raw:
    X.append([bin_income(income), bin_dti(dti), bin_history_months(hist), purpose])
    y.append(label)

X = np.array(X, dtype=object)
y = np.array(y, dtype=int)

# Split
idx = np.arange(len(X))
np.random.seed(2)
np.random.shuffle(idx)
train_idx, test_idx = idx[:8], idx[8:]

X_train, y_train = X[train_idx], y[train_idx]
X_test,  y_test  = X[test_idx],  y[test_idx]

model_credit = DiscreteNB_NoSK(alpha=1.0).fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = model_credit.predict(X_test)

cm, TP, FP, TN, FN = confusion_2x2(y_test, y_pred, positive_label=1)  # positive_label=GOOD
print("\n=== Credit Scoring P2P (No sklearn, Discrete NB) ===")
print("Confusion Matrix [[TN,FP],[FN,TP]] (TN=bad correctly):\n", cm)
print("Metrics (positive=GOOD):", metrics_from_cm(TP, FP, TN, FN))

# Prediksi calon borrower baru
new_applicants = np.array([
    [bin_income(11), bin_dti(0.55), bin_history_months(10), "working_capital"],  # agak risk
    [bin_income(14), bin_dti(0.18), bin_history_months(30), "investment"],      # bagus
], dtype=object)

print("Pred (1=GOOD,0=BAD):", model_credit.predict(new_applicants))
print("Proba [P(BAD), P(GOOD)] per row:\n", model_credit.predict_proba(new_applicants))
```

---

# B) DENGAN sklearn

> Catatan: untuk teks kita pakai **CountVectorizer + MultinomialNB**.
> Untuk credit scoring tabular, paling simpel: **OneHotEncoder + GaussianNB** (atau BernoulliNB). GaussianNB bekerja cukup baik setelah one-hot + numeric scaling sederhana.

## B1) (1) Spam Detection — sklearn

```python
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report

spam_texts = [
    "Gratis hadiah besar klik link sekarang",
    "Promo diskon 90 persen hanya hari ini",
    "Selamat kamu menang voucher belanja",
    "Pinjaman cepat cair tanpa syarat klik",
    "Dapatkan uang gratis sekarang juga",
    "Claim hadiah kamu melalui link ini",
]
ham_texts = [
    "Rapat jam 10 besok di ruang meeting",
    "Tolong kirim laporan mingguan hari ini",
    "Jadwal kuliah diundur jadi jam 13",
    "Terima kasih atas kerja samanya",
    "Mari diskusi proposal penelitian minggu depan",
    "Saya sudah upload materi ke LMS",
]

X = spam_texts + ham_texts
y = [1]*len(spam_texts) + [0]*len(ham_texts)

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.25, random_state=0, stratify=y)

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("vec", CountVectorizer(lowercase=True)),
    ("nb", MultinomialNB(alpha=1.0)),
])

pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = pipe.predict(X_test)

print("=== Spam Detection (sklearn) ===")
print("Confusion matrix [[TN,FP],[FN,TP]]:\n", confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred))
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred, digits=4))

samples = ["gratis voucher klik link", "mohon kirim revisi draft proposal"]
print("Pred:", pipe.predict(samples))
print("Proba:", pipe.predict_proba(samples))
```

---

## B2) (2) Sentiment Analysis — sklearn

```python
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report

pos = [
    "film ini bagus sekali dan sangat menyentuh",
    "saya suka layanan ini cepat dan ramah",
    "produk berkualitas dan pengiriman tepat waktu",
    "pengalaman luar biasa saya puas",
    "rekomendasi terbaik mantap",
]
neg = [
    "film ini buruk dan membosankan",
    "layanan lambat dan tidak membantu",
    "produk rusak dan mengecewakan",
    "pengalaman jelek saya kesal",
    "sangat tidak puas mengecewakan",
]

X = pos + neg
y = [1]*len(pos) + [0]*len(neg)

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.30, random_state=1, stratify=y)

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("vec", CountVectorizer(lowercase=True, ngram_range=(1,2))),  # bigram sering bantu sentiment
    ("nb", MultinomialNB(alpha=1.0)),
])

pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = pipe.predict(X_test)

print("=== Sentiment Analysis (sklearn) ===")
print("Confusion matrix [[TN,FP],[FN,TP]]:\n", confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred))
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred, digits=4))

samples = ["saya puas sekali layanan ramah", "produk ini mengecewakan dan buruk"]
print("Pred:", pipe.predict(samples))
print("Proba:", pipe.predict_proba(samples))
```

---

## B3) (3) Credit Scoring P2P — sklearn (OneHot + GaussianNB)

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import OneHotEncoder
from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report
from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer

# Data demo: [income_juta, dti, history_months, purpose]
raw = [
    [10, 0.25, 18, "working_capital", 1],
    [3,  0.60, 4,  "consumption",     0],
    [7,  0.35, 8,  "education",       1],
    [4,  0.55, 10, "consumption",     0],
    [15, 0.15, 36, "investment",      1],
    [6,  0.70, 5,  "working_capital", 0],
    [9,  0.40, 30, "working_capital", 1],
    [2,  0.80, 3,  "consumption",     0],
    [13, 0.30, 20, "investment",      1],
    [5,  0.45, 12, "education",       1],
    [8,  0.65, 7,  "consumption",     0],
    [12, 0.22, 28, "working_capital", 1],
]

raw = np.array(raw, dtype=object)
X = raw[:, :4]
y = raw[:, 4].astype(int)

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size=0.30, random_state=2, stratify=y)

# Kolom: 0 income (num), 1 dti (num), 2 history (num), 3 purpose (cat)
num_cols = [0,1,2]
cat_cols = [3]

preprocess = ColumnTransformer(
    transformers=[
        ("num", Pipeline([("imp", SimpleImputer(strategy="median"))]), num_cols),
        ("cat", Pipeline([("imp", SimpleImputer(strategy="most_frequent")),
                          ("oh", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown="ignore"))]), cat_cols),
    ],
    remainder="drop"
)

# GaussianNB butuh array dense (karena output OneHot sparse)
class DenseGaussianNB(GaussianNB):
    def fit(self, X, y):
        return super().fit(X.toarray() if hasattr(X, "toarray") else X, y)
    def predict(self, X):
        Xd = X.toarray() if hasattr(X, "toarray") else X
        return super().predict(Xd)
    def predict_proba(self, X):
        Xd = X.toarray() if hasattr(X, "toarray") else X
        return super().predict_proba(Xd)

pipe = Pipeline([
    ("prep", preprocess),
    ("nb", DenseGaussianNB())
])

pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)
y_pred = pipe.predict(X_test)

print("=== Credit Scoring P2P (sklearn) ===")
print("Confusion matrix [[TN,FP],[FN,TP]]:\n", confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred))
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred, digits=4))

new_applicants = np.array([
    [11, 0.55, 10, "working_capital"],
    [14, 0.18, 30, "investment"],
], dtype=object)

print("Pred (1=GOOD,0=BAD):", pipe.predict(new_applicants))
print("Proba [P(BAD),P(GOOD)]:\n", pipe.predict_proba(new_applicants))
```

---

## Catatan singkat untuk penggunaan di kelas (biar “nyambung” dengan Bayesian update)

* **Prior** = proporsi kelas (spam rate / positive sentiment rate / default rate).
* **Likelihood** (teks) = frekuensi kata per kelas.
* **Imbalance** (mis. default rate kecil) → **precision bisa jeblok** walau “akurasi tinggi”.

Kalau Bapak mau, saya bisa:

* menambah versi **log-likelihood ratio** yang eksplisit (lebih “Sherlock Holmes 2.0”), atau
* menyiapkan **starter repo GitHub Classroom**: `src/nb.py`, `train.py`, `evaluate.py`, `tests/` untuk autograding.


Berikut **versi Log-Likelihood Ratio (LLR)** yang “Sherlock Holmes banget”: kita tidak hitung (P(y|x)) langsung, tapi hitung **log-odds**:

[
\log \frac{P(y=1\mid x)}{P(y=0\mid x)}
======================================

\log \frac{P(y=1)}{P(y=0)}
+
\log \frac{P(x\mid y=1)}{P(x\mid y=0)}
]

Keputusan:

* jika **LLR > 0** ⇒ pilih kelas 1
* jika **LLR < 0** ⇒ pilih kelas 0
  Ambang bisa digeser (mis. untuk mengurangi false positive).

Di bawah ini saya buat **3 aplikasi** dan **2 versi kode**:

* **A) tanpa sklearn**
* **B) dengan sklearn**

---

# A) TANPA sklearn

## A0) Utility: prior-odds, sigmoid (opsional)

```python
import numpy as np

def logit(p):
    p = float(p)
    p = min(max(p, 1e-15), 1-1e-15)
    return np.log(p/(1-p))

def sigmoid(z):
    return 1/(1+np.exp(-z))
```

---

## A1) LLR untuk TEKS (Multinomial NB dengan counts)

LLR dokumen untuk kelas 1 vs 0:

[
\text{LLR}(d)=\log\frac{P(y=1)}{P(y=0)}+\sum_w c(w,d)\cdot \log\frac{P(w\mid 1)}{P(w\mid 0)}
]

### Engine: fit → score_llr → predict

```python
import re
from collections import Counter

def tokenize(text):
    return re.findall(r"[a-zA-Z0-9]+", text.lower())

class TextLLR_NoSK:
    """
    Multinomial NB versi LLR untuk binary classification.
    label 1 = positive/spam, label 0 = negative/ham
    """
    def __init__(self, alpha=1.0):
        self.alpha = float(alpha)
        self.vocab = set()
        self.log_prior_odds = 0.0
        self.log_pw_given_1 = {}
        self.log_pw_given_0 = {}
        self.log_ratio_w = {}   # log P(w|1) - log P(w|0)
        self.log_unk_ratio = 0.0

    def fit(self, texts, y):
        texts = list(texts)
        y = np.asarray(y).astype(int)

        # prior odds
        p1 = y.mean()
        self.log_prior_odds = logit(p1)

        # count words per class
        c1 = Counter()
        c0 = Counter()
        n1 = 0
        n0 = 0
        for t, label in zip(texts, y):
            words = tokenize(t)
            if label == 1:
                c1.update(words); n1 += 1
            else:
                c0.update(words); n0 += 1
            self.vocab.update(words)

        V = len(self.vocab)

        total1 = sum(c1.values())
        total0 = sum(c0.values())

        denom1 = total1 + self.alpha * V
        denom0 = total0 + self.alpha * V

        # precompute log probs and log ratios
        self.log_pw_given_1 = {}
        self.log_pw_given_0 = {}
        self.log_ratio_w = {}

        for w in self.vocab:
            p_w_1 = (c1[w] + self.alpha) / denom1
            p_w_0 = (c0[w] + self.alpha) / denom0
            self.log_pw_given_1[w] = np.log(p_w_1)
            self.log_pw_given_0[w] = np.log(p_w_0)
            self.log_ratio_w[w] = self.log_pw_given_1[w] - self.log_pw_given_0[w]

        # unseen word ratio
        p_unk_1 = self.alpha / denom1
        p_unk_0 = self.alpha / denom0
        self.log_unk_ratio = np.log(p_unk_1) - np.log(p_unk_0)
        return self

    def score_llr(self, text):
        counts = Counter(tokenize(text))
        s = self.log_prior_odds
        for w, k in counts.items():
            s += k * self.log_ratio_w.get(w, self.log_unk_ratio)
        return float(s)

    def predict(self, texts, threshold=0.0):
        scores = np.array([self.score_llr(t) for t in texts], dtype=float)
        return (scores > threshold).astype(int), scores

    def predict_proba(self, texts, threshold=0.0):
        # P(y=1|x) = sigmoid(LLR - threshold_shift)
        scores = np.array([self.score_llr(t) for t in texts], dtype=float)
        z = scores - threshold
        p1 = sigmoid(z)
        return np.vstack([1-p1, p1]).T, scores
```

---

## A1a) (1) Spam Detection (LLR)

```python
spam = [
    "Gratis hadiah besar klik link sekarang",
    "Promo diskon 90 persen hanya hari ini",
    "Selamat kamu menang voucher belanja",
    "Pinjaman cepat cair tanpa syarat klik",
]
ham = [
    "Rapat jam 10 besok di ruang meeting",
    "Tolong kirim laporan mingguan hari ini",
    "Jadwal kuliah diundur jadi jam 13",
    "Saya sudah upload materi ke LMS",
]

X = spam + ham
y = [1]*len(spam) + [0]*len(ham)

model = TextLLR_NoSK(alpha=1.0).fit(X, y)

tests = [
    "gratis voucher klik link",
    "mohon kirim revisi draft proposal",
    "promo diskon besar terbatas",
]
pred, llr = model.predict(tests, threshold=0.0)
proba, _ = model.predict_proba(tests)

for t, p, s, pr in zip(tests, pred, llr, proba):
    print(f"\nTEXT: {t}\nLLR={s:.3f}  pred={p}  P(spam)={pr[1]:.3f}")
```

> **Geser threshold** untuk mengurangi false positive: mis. `threshold=1.5` (lebih ketat memanggil spam).

---

## A1b) (2) Sentiment Analysis (LLR)

```python
pos = [
    "film ini bagus sekali dan sangat menyentuh",
    "saya suka layanan ini cepat dan ramah",
    "pengalaman luar biasa saya puas",
]
neg = [
    "film ini buruk dan membosankan",
    "layanan lambat dan tidak membantu",
    "pengalaman jelek saya kesal",
]

X = pos + neg
y = [1]*len(pos) + [0]*len(neg)

model = TextLLR_NoSK(alpha=1.0).fit(X, y)

tests = [
    "saya puas sekali layanan ramah",
    "produk ini mengecewakan dan buruk",
]
pred, llr = model.predict(tests)
proba, _ = model.predict_proba(tests)
for t, p, s, pr in zip(tests, pred, llr, proba):
    print(f"\nTEXT: {t}\nLLR={s:.3f}  pred={p}  P(positive)={pr[1]:.3f}")
```

---

## A2) (3) Credit Scoring P2P (LLR diskret per-fitur)

Untuk fitur kategori/diskret:

[
\text{LLR}(x)=\log\frac{P(GOOD)}{P(BAD)}+\sum_j \log\frac{P(x_j=v_j\mid GOOD)}{P(x_j=v_j\mid BAD)}
]

### Engine: fit dari X kategori

```python
from collections import Counter, defaultdict

class DiscreteLLR_NoSK:
    """
    Binary discrete NB, output skor LLR.
    y=1: GOOD (lancar), y=0: BAD (default)
    """
    def __init__(self, alpha=1.0):
        self.alpha = float(alpha)
        self.log_prior_odds = 0.0
        self.feature_vals = []
        self.log_ratio = []      # list[dict[val] -> log P(val|1)-log P(val|0)]
        self.log_unk_ratio = []  # per fitur

    def fit(self, X, y):
        X = np.asarray(X, dtype=object)
        y = np.asarray(y).astype(int)
        n, d = X.shape

        self.log_prior_odds = logit(y.mean())
        self.feature_vals = [sorted(set(X[:, j])) for j in range(d)]
        self.log_ratio = [dict() for _ in range(d)]
        self.log_unk_ratio = [0.0 for _ in range(d)]

        X1 = X[y == 1]
        X0 = X[y == 0]

        for j in range(d):
            vals = self.feature_vals[j]
            V = len(vals)

            c1 = Counter(X1[:, j]) if len(X1) else Counter()
            c0 = Counter(X0[:, j]) if len(X0) else Counter()

            denom1 = len(X1) + self.alpha * V
            denom0 = len(X0) + self.alpha * V

            for v in vals:
                p1 = (c1.get(v, 0) + self.alpha) / denom1
                p0 = (c0.get(v, 0) + self.alpha) / denom0
                self.log_ratio[j][v] = np.log(p1) - np.log(p0)

            # unseen ratio
            self.log_unk_ratio[j] = np.log(self.alpha/denom1) - np.log(self.alpha/denom0)

        return self

    def score_llr_row(self, row):
        s = self.log_prior_odds
        for j, v in enumerate(row):
            s += self.log_ratio[j].get(v, self.log_unk_ratio[j])
        return float(s)

    def predict(self, X, threshold=0.0):
        X = np.asarray(X, dtype=object)
        scores = np.array([self.score_llr_row(r) for r in X], dtype=float)
        yhat = (scores > threshold).astype(int)
        return yhat, scores

    def predict_proba(self, X, threshold=0.0):
        yhat, scores = self.predict(X, threshold=threshold)
        p1 = sigmoid(scores - threshold)
        return np.vstack([1-p1, p1]).T, scores
```

### Demo P2P (pakai binning seperti sebelumnya)

```python
def bin_income(x):
    if x < 5: return "low"
    if x < 12: return "mid"
    return "high"

def bin_dti(x):
    if x < 0.2: return "low"
    if x < 0.5: return "mid"
    return "high"

def bin_history(m):
    if m < 6: return "short"
    if m < 24: return "mid"
    return "long"

raw = [
    (10, 0.25, 18, "working_capital", 1),
    (3,  0.60, 4,  "consumption",     0),
    (7,  0.35, 8,  "education",       1),
    (4,  0.55, 10, "consumption",     0),
    (15, 0.15, 36, "investment",      1),
    (6,  0.70, 5,  "working_capital", 0),
    (9,  0.40, 30, "working_capital", 1),
    (2,  0.80, 3,  "consumption",     0),
]

X = []
y = []
for income, dti, hist, purpose, label in raw:
    X.append([bin_income(income), bin_dti(dti), bin_history(hist), purpose])
    y.append(label)

X = np.array(X, dtype=object)
y = np.array(y, dtype=int)

model = DiscreteLLR_NoSK(alpha=1.0).fit(X, y)

new_apps = np.array([
    [bin_income(11), bin_dti(0.55), bin_history(10), "working_capital"],
    [bin_income(14), bin_dti(0.18), bin_history(30), "investment"],
], dtype=object)

pred, llr = model.predict(new_apps, threshold=0.0)
proba, _ = model.predict_proba(new_apps)

for r, p, s, pr in zip(new_apps, pred, llr, proba):
    print(f"\nX={r}\nLLR={s:.3f}  pred(GOOD=1)={p}  P(GOOD)={pr[1]:.3f}")
```

---

# B) DENGAN sklearn (LLR dari parameter NB)

Intinya: sklearn sudah menghitung **joint log-likelihood** per kelas.
Untuk binary, LLR = (log joint kelas 1) − (log joint kelas 0).

## B1) LLR untuk teks dengan sklearn (CountVectorizer + MultinomialNB)

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB

def llr_text_sklearn(texts_train, y_train, texts_test, alpha=1.0):
    vec = CountVectorizer(lowercase=True)
    Xtr = vec.fit_transform(texts_train)

    nb = MultinomialNB(alpha=alpha)
    nb.fit(Xtr, y_train)

    Xte = vec.transform(texts_test)

    # joint log-likelihood per kelas: shape (n_samples, 2)
    jll = nb._joint_log_likelihood(Xte)

    # asumsikan kelas bernilai [0,1]
    # LLR = log P(x,y=1) - log P(x,y=0)
    llr = jll[:, 1] - jll[:, 0]
    pred = (llr > 0).astype(int)

    # opsional: probabilitas via sigmoid(llr) (untuk binary NB, ini konsisten)
    p1 = sigmoid(llr)
    proba = np.vstack([1-p1, p1]).T

    return pred, llr, proba, vec, nb

# Demo spam
spam = [
    "Gratis hadiah besar klik link sekarang",
    "Promo diskon 90 persen hanya hari ini",
    "Selamat kamu menang voucher belanja",
    "Pinjaman cepat cair tanpa syarat klik",
]
ham = [
    "Rapat jam 10 besok di ruang meeting",
    "Tolong kirim laporan mingguan hari ini",
    "Jadwal kuliah diundur jadi jam 13",
    "Saya sudah upload materi ke LMS",
]
Xtr = spam + ham
ytr = [1]*len(spam) + [0]*len(ham)

tests = ["gratis voucher klik link", "mohon kirim revisi draft proposal"]

pred, llr, proba, vec, nb = llr_text_sklearn(Xtr, ytr, tests, alpha=1.0)
for t, p, s, pr in zip(tests, pred, llr, proba):
    print(f"\nTEXT: {t}\nLLR={s:.3f}  pred={p}  P(class1)={pr[1]:.3f}")
```

> Untuk **sentiment**, tinggal ganti data latihnya; engine sama.

---

## B2) LLR untuk credit scoring tabular dengan sklearn

Pilihan yang rapi untuk LLR adalah **CategoricalNB** (fitur kategori berupa integer).
Alurnya: encode kategori → `CategoricalNB` → ambil `_joint_log_likelihood` → LLR.

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.naive_bayes import CategoricalNB
from sklearn.preprocessing import OrdinalEncoder

def llr_credit_categorical_sklearn(X_train, y_train, X_test, alpha=1.0):
    enc = OrdinalEncoder(handle_unknown="use_encoded_value", unknown_value=-1)
    Xtr_enc = enc.fit_transform(X_train)
    Xte_enc = enc.transform(X_test)

    nb = CategoricalNB(alpha=alpha)
    nb.fit(Xtr_enc, y_train)

    jll = nb._joint_log_likelihood(Xte_enc)
    llr = jll[:, 1] - jll[:, 0]
    pred = (llr > 0).astype(int)

    p1 = sigmoid(llr)
    proba = np.vstack([1-p1, p1]).T
    return pred, llr, proba, enc, nb

# Demo P2P kategori (pakai binning)
def bin_income(x):
    if x < 5: return "low"
    if x < 12: return "mid"
    return "high"

def bin_dti(x):
    if x < 0.2: return "low"
    if x < 0.5: return "mid"
    return "high"

def bin_history(m):
    if m < 6: return "short"
    if m < 24: return "mid"
    return "long"

raw = [
    (10, 0.25, 18, "working_capital", 1),
    (3,  0.60, 4,  "consumption",     0),
    (7,  0.35, 8,  "education",       1),
    (4,  0.55, 10, "consumption",     0),
    (15, 0.15, 36, "investment",      1),
    (6,  0.70, 5,  "working_capital", 0),
    (9,  0.40, 30, "working_capital", 1),
    (2,  0.80, 3,  "consumption",     0),
]

X = []
y = []
for income, dti, hist, purpose, label in raw:
    X.append([bin_income(income), bin_dti(dti), bin_history(hist), purpose])
    y.append(label)

X = np.array(X, dtype=object)
y = np.array(y, dtype=int)

new_apps = np.array([
    [bin_income(11), bin_dti(0.55), bin_history(10), "working_capital"],
    [bin_income(14), bin_dti(0.18), bin_history(30), "investment"],
], dtype=object)

pred, llr, proba, enc, nb = llr_credit_categorical_sklearn(X, y, new_apps, alpha=1.0)

for r, p, s, pr in zip(new_apps, pred, llr, proba):
    print(f"\nX={r}\nLLR={s:.3f}  pred(GOOD=1)={p}  P(GOOD)={pr[1]:.3f}")
```

---

## Inti pedagogis yang biasanya “nendang” di kelas

* **LLR = bukti (evidence) dalam satu angka**.
* **Prior** menggeser baseline log-odds.
* Setiap fitur/kata memberi kontribusi:
  (\log\frac{P(\text{fitur}|1)}{P(\text{fitur}|0)}) = “seberapa kuat fitur ini mendukung kelas 1”.

Kalau Bapak mau, saya bisa tambahkan fungsi `explain(text)` yang mengurutkan **top-k kata** penyumbang LLR terbesar (fit untuk demo “Sherlock reasoning”).
